title: The One with Princess Consuela

jokes.text: Because every time we do you make jokes about swinging and scare them away.
jokes.setup: Chandler questions why they don't hang out with married couples more often
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals his inappropriate behavior ruins it
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor that reveals Chandler's social awkwardness through Monica's exasperation

jokes.text: You mean that Portuguese couple? Yeah, like you wouldn't have done it.
jokes.punchline: Chandler doubles down, suggesting Monica would have participated
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that turns the tables on Monica while staying true to Chandler's defensive humor

jokes.text: A dinosaur died a million years ago?
jokes.setup: Ross asks to guess what happened at work
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic paleontology joke
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration - only Chandler would deflate Ross's excitement this way, with perfect economy

jokes.text: Try 65 million years ago.
jokes.punchline: Ross earnestly corrects the timeline while missing the sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross characterization - his obliviousness to sarcasm when it involves his expertise

jokes.text: Yeah, do you have any idea what this means in academic circles? I am gonna get laid.
jokes.setup: Serious tone about academic achievement meaning
jokes.punchline: Crude punchline about sexual benefits
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection structure but somewhat predictable punchline for Ross

jokes.text: Oh, Italian. No one-- No one wanted seconds, right?
jokes.punchline: She awkwardly assumes they're talking about food
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness humor from misreading the room

jokes.text: What are the odds?
jokes.setup: Both announce good career news simultaneously
jokes.punchline: Ross's oblivious question about the coincidence
jokes.explanation: Light observational humor about coincidental timing

jokes.text: I finally got that seed out of my teeth.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Ross announce major career developments
jokes.punchline: Joey contributes trivial personal achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his complete lack of awareness of appropriate social context, delivered with genuine pride

jokes.text: I don't know who I'm happiest for. / I do. He's been working on that all day.
jokes.setup: Monica's polite confusion about equal celebration
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately identifies Joey's achievement as most significant
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that elevates Joey's trivial achievement over genuine career milestones through Chandler's deadpan logic

jokes.text: The Ministry of Names... Bureau.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks where she went to change her name
jokes.punchline: Monica invents an official-sounding but ridiculous bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Monica's attempt to sound official while clearly making it up, fits her perfectionist character struggling with dishonesty

jokes.text: You never did it.
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately calls out her obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Clean puncture of Monica's deception with perfect economy and Chandler's characteristic directness

jokes.text: It's just the idea of being an official Bing.
jokes.setup: Monica's reluctance to change her name
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Bing' as inherently embarrassing official status
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's social awareness of the Bing name's comedic associations

jokes.text: Who am I kidding? Let's call the kid Geller and let Bing die with me.
jokes.punchline: Immediately surrendering and accepting his name's doom
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor that's both dramatically overblown and perfectly in character for Chandler's self-awareness

jokes.text: Call me Mrs. Hannigan. / Mrs. Hannigan? Can't you see I'm in the middle of something?
jokes.setup: Phoebe testing out the married name
jokes.punchline: Mike instantly roleplays the Annie orphanage matron
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that's instantly recognizable and well-executed, showing Mike's quick wit

jokes.text: No, it's not close. You said it was in escrow. I couldn't even find that on the map.
jokes.setup: Friends saying the house is close
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks 'escrow' is a geographical location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey malapropism - believable confusion that's both endearing and ridiculous, with great delivery setup

jokes.text: No, Joey. Escrow is... There's money, and... It's not the bank exactly. I don't know what it is.
jokes.punchline: Ross, the PhD, completely fails to explain basic concept
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where the educated character is equally confused, showing Ross's expertise doesn't extend to practical matters

jokes.text: The only reason I'm going to their stupid house is to point out everything wrong with it so they don't move.
jokes.punchline: Reveals his actual sabotage plan
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Joey's emotional immaturity beneath his good intentions

jokes.text: You're a strange kind of grownup.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's perfectly understated assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe wisdom delivered with characteristic bluntness and perfect economy

jokes.text: There's something I've been trying to get Mike to do in bed, and-- / Can we not talk about that now? / All right. Prude.
jokes.setup: Phoebe about to share intimate details as example
jokes.punchline: Joey's discomfort followed by Phoebe calling him a prude
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal where typically forward Joey is the uncomfortable one about sex talk

jokes.text: I bet I could even get Mike to do that thing. What is it?
jokes.setup: Joey boasting about his persuasive abilities
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking for sexual details he just rejected
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey's curiosity overriding his earlier prudishness

jokes.text: It's spelled like 'gucky,' which can be confusing.
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing luxury brand as something gross
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - shows Rachel's nervousness and creates genuine phonetic humor

jokes.text: Oh, my God. That's my boss. You have to seat us somewhere else.
jokes.setup: Rachel at job interview in restaurant
jokes.punchline: Boss appears at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for cringe comedy but well-executed situational irony

jokes.text: I know. With 'gucky.'
jokes.punchline: Hostess matter-of-factly using Rachel's wrong pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the mispronunciation has stuck, delivered with perfect deadpan acceptance

jokes.text: I'm on a date.
jokes.setup: Boss discovering Rachel at restaurant with man
jokes.punchline: Rachel's panicked lie making situation worse
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe escalation where the lie creates bigger problems than the truth

jokes.text: Thank you, JewHunks.com, huh?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate dating site reference
jokes.explanation: Awkward attempt at humor that feels slightly forced and relies on ethnic reference for shock value

jokes.text: I wouldn't call my online dating profile a résumé.
jokes.punchline: Rachel thinking he's referring to dating profile
jokes.explanation: Good confusion comedy showing how Rachel's lie is creating communication problems

jokes.text: Whatever happened to just singing for no reason?
jokes.punchline: Rachel's random nostalgic non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Quirky moment that feels authentically random while showing Rachel's mental state under pressure

jokes.text: Maybe people found it weird.
jokes.punchline: Interviewer's dry, logical response
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that punctures Rachel's whimsy with practical reality

jokes.text: I believe what she's trying to tell you is that I'm her boss.
jokes.setup: Confused conversation between Rachel and interviewer
jokes.punchline: Boss revealing himself and Rachel's deception
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe payoff where the deception collapses, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Mr. Boss, it is a pleasure to meet you.
jokes.setup: Meeting Rachel's boss in awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Addressing him as 'Mr. Boss' like it's his name
jokes.explanation: Good awkward formality that shows how the situation has everyone confused about proper etiquette

jokes.text: This place is so depressing. If I had to work here, I'd kill myself. But you obviously haven't.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded observation about Ross's continued existence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic that starts dark but becomes accidentally complimentary through twisted reasoning

jokes.text: See, I need to change it because I'm hiding from the law.
jokes.setup: Clerk asking why she wants to change her name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casually criminal explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - casually mentioning something alarming as if it's mundane, delivered with characteristic directness

jokes.text: You're fun.
jokes.punchline: Clerk's understated positive response
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that treats Phoebe's weirdness as refreshingly entertaining rather than concerning

jokes.text: Well, not anything. / Yeah. Anything.
jokes.punchline: Clerk confirming there really are no limits
jokes.explanation: Setup for the bigger payoff coming, establishing the absurd freedom Phoebe has

jokes.text: Get out of my line.
jokes.setup: Phoebe taking too long with decision
jokes.punchline: Clerk's blunt dismissal after being so accommodating
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where the patient clerk suddenly has limits, delivered with perfect bureaucratic authority

jokes.text: Oh, not anymore. I changed it today. / Oh, I'm sorry, Mrs. Hannigan. / Wrong again.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to guess Phoebe's new name
jokes.punchline: Multiple wrong guesses building anticipation
jokes.explanation: Good setup building tension for the big reveal, with Monica's polite confusion

jokes.text: So meet Princess Consuela Bananahammock.
jokes.setup: Building anticipation for Phoebe's new name
jokes.punchline: Completely ridiculous combination of regal and crude
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that perfectly captures Phoebe's absurdist logic - combining dignity with complete inappropriateness. Became a cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: That's what we were gonna name the baby.
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact claim about baby naming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - deflating the absurdity with an even more absurd claim delivered completely straight

jokes.text: No, I'm gonna have my friends call me Valerie.
jokes.setup: Being asked if they should call her Princess Consuela
jokes.punchline: Choosing a completely ordinary name instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurdity - going through elaborate name change only to use something completely mundane for daily use

jokes.text: Well, I didn't get the job at Gucci and I got fired from Ralph Lauren.
jokes.punchline: Complete disaster with double failure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where things go worse than anyone expected, delivered with Rachel's characteristic dramatic efficiency

jokes.text: That is a bad interview.
jokes.punchline: Joey's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey understatement that highlights the magnitude of disaster through massive underreaction

jokes.text: And I said, 'Wait a minute. Yes, I am.' And then I had to sit there for 45 minutes while he proved that that, in fact, was true.
jokes.setup: Boss calling Rachel not a team player
jokes.punchline: Her defense backfiring spectacularly with evidence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where Rachel's attempt to defend herself leads to detailed documentation of her failures

jokes.text: You want me to join you? / I thought Joey was here. Five is good.
jokes.setup: Ross ordering six glasses, Gunther asking to join
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately rescinding invitation upon realization
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's social awkwardness and Gunther's painful earnestness

jokes.text: Wow, I'm gonna have a loogey in my coffee tomorrow.
jokes.punchline: Ross realizing consequences for his daily coffee
jokes.explanation: Good consequence humor showing Ross's delayed social awareness and practical concerns

jokes.text: Israeli champagne. And it's vanilla.
jokes.punchline: Two equally disappointing qualities
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about cheap celebration, with Monica's characteristic attention to quality details

jokes.text: I got tenure. I didn't win the lottery.
jokes.punchline: Ross's realistic assessment of his achievement's value
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor where Ross acknowledges the practical limitations of academic success

jokes.text: And to knowing that your career doesn't mean everything. But also knowing it means a lot. But more importantly, to full, well-rounded lives. That center around work.
jokes.punchline: Contradicting herself multiple times in single toast
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's inability to commit to any philosophical position, building contradictions nicely

jokes.text: You know what the best part about this is? I can never be fired.
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing job security right before learning Rachel was fired
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing that sets up the devastating contrast with Rachel's situation

jokes.text: Little heads-up would've been nice.
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate job security celebration while Rachel was fired
jokes.punchline: Ross's massive understatement of his social failure
jokes.explanation: Great Ross moment showing his complete inability to gauge social situations, delivered with perfect awkwardness

jokes.text: So happy for you guys. Although, you know, I hope you like fungus.
jokes.setup: Joey pretending to be supportive of house purchase
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to sabotage with fake problem
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's sabotage plan, showing his inability to maintain the facade for even one sentence

jokes.text: Okay. Then I guess I have dry eyes and a scratchy throat for no reason.
jokes.setup: Being told there's no fungus in house
jokes.punchline: Joey doubling down with fake symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Joey commits to his fake health problems to maintain the sabotage

jokes.text: Maybe it's because you hung your head out the window like a dog the whole ride here.
jokes.setup: Joey's fake symptoms from house fungus
jokes.punchline: Chandler revealing the real cause of Joey's condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy reference that completely deflates Joey's sabotage while creating vivid mental image

jokes.text: I think I just saw a bat in the corner.
jokes.punchline: Adding increasingly dramatic problems
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of Joey's sabotage, predictable but in character

jokes.text: When your head was hanging out the window, it didn't hit a mailbox, did it?
jokes.punchline: Monica suggesting brain damage from car ride
jokes.explanation: Great callback that escalates the physical comedy while providing logical explanation for Joey's behavior

jokes.text: I hope you get used to that weird humming sound.
jokes.punchline: Creating non-existent problem on the spot
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Joey's increasingly desperate sabotage attempts

jokes.text: Joey, we know that's you. / No... it's not.
jokes.setup: Joey making humming sound to fake house problem
jokes.punchline: Getting caught and weakly denying it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff where Joey's sabotage is transparently obvious but he still tries to maintain it

jokes.text: Meet Princess Consuela Bananahammock. / You're kidding, right? / Nope.
jokes.setup: Phoebe revealing her name change to Mike
jokes.punchline: Mike's disbelief and Phoebe's casual confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Mike's normal reaction to Phoebe's absurdity, with her characteristic nonchalance

jokes.text: Then I'm gonna change my name. / Great. Okay, what are you gonna change it to? / Crap Bag.
jokes.setup: Mike threatening to change his name in response
jokes.punchline: Choosing the most deliberately awful name possible
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that matches Phoebe's absurdity while being even more deliberately offensive and crude

jokes.text: Mike Crap Bag? / No. No 'Mike.' No, just 'Crap Bag.' First name: Crap. Last name: Bag.
jokes.setup: Phoebe assuming he'd keep his first name
jokes.punchline: Mike going completely overboard with the commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where Mike commits fully to the absurd choice, with methodical explanation that makes it even funnier

jokes.text: It's fun, it's different, and no one else has a name like that.
jokes.punchline: Using Phoebe's exact logic for terrible name
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Mike turning Phoebe's logic against her with perfect mimicry of her reasoning

jokes.text: And I love your name. I love Princess Consuela. And I love Crap.
jokes.punchline: Final word creates unintended crude meaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination where Phoebe's sweet sentiment ends with unintentionally crude declaration

jokes.text: Oh, hi. I'm Joey. My stupid friends are buying this house. Who are you? / I'm Mackenzie. My stupid parents are selling this house.
jokes.setup: Joey meeting girl at the house
jokes.punchline: Perfect parallel complaints about the adults
jokes.explanation: Great parallel structure that immediately establishes common ground between Joey and child through shared frustration

jokes.text: I hate my parents. / I hate my friends.
jokes.punchline: Escalating to dramatic mutual hatred
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Joey bonding with a child at her maturity level

jokes.text: Okay, you come with me and you tell them that the house is haunted. / What are you, 8?
jokes.punchline: Eight-year-old calling out adult's childish plan
jokes.explanation: Excellent role reversal where the child is more mature than Joey, delivered with perfect incredulous tone

jokes.text: Okay. Let's hear your great idea. / I don't have any great ideas. I am 8.
jokes.setup: Joey challenging her to do better
jokes.punchline: Child pointing out her age-appropriate limitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic that highlights the absurdity of an adult expecting strategic planning from a child

jokes.text: This is what my mom was talking about. Whiners are wieners.
jokes.punchline: Child quoting parental wisdom with alliteration
jokes.explanation: Good child wisdom that's both silly and actually insightful, with memorable wordplay

jokes.text: How'd you get to be so smart? / I read a lot. / Just when I thought we could be friends.
jokes.punchline: Rejecting friendship over reading habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment where intellectual activity is a dealbreaker for friendship, completely in character

jokes.text: You still don't know my name, do you? / Well, now I don't have to.
jokes.setup: Rachel getting fired, Gunther offering sympathy
jokes.punchline: Gunther's logical response to unemployment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Gunther moment with perfect cruel logic delivered matter-of-factly, showing his petty side

jokes.text: I'm sorry. It's almost as if this wasn't built for a quick getaway.
jokes.setup: Ross taking long time with wheelchair/mobility device
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about design limitations
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about practical limitations, delivered with Ross's characteristic sarcasm

jokes.text: You know, you can't always get a seat on the subway, so....
jokes.setup: Mark asking about Ross's portable chair
jokes.punchline: Ross's ridiculous practical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic where Ross tries to make his embarrassing situation sound practical and clever

jokes.text: Clever.
jokes.punchline: Mark's politely sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Efficient sarcasm that punctures Ross's attempt to seem ingenious

jokes.text: Screw charity work. What do you got?
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she'll do charity work
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning altruism for job opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's practical priorities overriding her charitable pretenses

jokes.text: Nice to see you again. / Yeah. Yeah. I got tenure.
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward, irrelevant boast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross awkwardness where he randomly asserts his achievement in inappropriate context

jokes.text: Ross, that's Mark. From Bloomingdale's? You were insanely jealous of him.
jokes.punchline: Rachel reminding him of his obsessive jealousy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series history that re-establishes the dynamic and Ross's embarrassment

jokes.text: Maybe make you his 'sex-cretary.'
jokes.punchline: Crude portmanteau showing his paranoia
jokes.explanation: Serviceable wordplay that shows Ross's jealous mindset, but fairly predictable pun

jokes.text: Man, those two dogs are going at it.
jokes.punchline: Noting dogs mating in perfect suburban setting
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor that undercuts the suburban ideal with natural animal behavior

jokes.text: Joey? There was a little girl who lived here, but she died, like, 30 years ago. / What? / I'm just messing with you.
jokes.punchline: Monica's fake ghost story followed by reveal
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection that plays on Joey's fears but gets deflated quickly

jokes.text: That's not funny. You know I'm afraid of little-girl ghosts.
jokes.punchline: Joey's very specific supernatural fear
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that's both specific and absurd - why specifically little-girl ghosts? Very Joey logic

jokes.text: You don't think we'd buy a house and not have a Joey room, do you?
jokes.setup: Joey surprised about having his own room
jokes.punchline: Treating Joey room as obvious necessity
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that shows how integral Joey is to their lives, treating his space as non-negotiable

jokes.text: Hey, can I have an aquarium? And a sex swing? / No. / Why not? I'll keep the tank clean.
jokes.punchline: Clarifying which item he'll maintain properly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey misdirection where he clarifies maintenance commitment for the innocent item, implying the other needs no care

jokes.text: This is my husband, Crap Bag.
jokes.setup: Phoebe introducing Mike to massage client
jokes.punchline: Actually using the ridiculous name in public
jokes.explanation: Great payoff showing the real-world consequences of their absurd name game

jokes.text: If you need an easy way to remember it, just think of a bag of crap.
jokes.punchline: Mike helpfully making it even more crude
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where Mike's attempt to be helpful makes the situation worse with vivid imagery

jokes.text: How about Buffay-Hannigan? / Really? / Yeah. I'm Phoebe Buffay-Hannigan-Bananahammock.
jokes.punchline: Adding Bananahammock to the reasonable combination
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where Phoebe's reasonable compromise gets ruined by her inability to let go of the absurd element

jokes.text: Do you even know what a banana hammock is? / It's a funny word. / It's a Speedo.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Phoebe's innocent ignorance of crude meaning
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that explains Phoebe's choice while maintaining her innocent character despite crude language

jokes.text: Oh, crap.
jokes.punchline: Accidental reference to Mike's chosen name
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy where Phoebe's natural expletive accidentally references the name game, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: The two dogs.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what he's thinking about
jokes.punchline: Still fixated on the mating dogs from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Chandler's mind wandering to the inappropriate moment from earlier

jokes.text: My time machine works.
jokes.setup: Chandler noting it's seven years ago scenario
jokes.punchline: Ross's ridiculous explanation for timeline
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor that acknowledges the repetitive nature of Ross's jealousy through absurd sci-fi logic

jokes.text: Excuse me? / Oh, please. Yesterday, on the subway, you couldn't stop staring at that woman with the big breasts. / For your information, I was staring at her baby.
jokes.setup: Monica accusing Chandler of ogling woman
jokes.punchline: Chandler's innocent explanation with pregnancy context
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that recontextualizes apparently inappropriate behavior with sweet motivation

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Yeah, I wonder why? What could that smarmy letch possibly want?
jokes.setup: Rachel saying Mark was sweet at dinner
jokes.punchline: Ross's dramatic overreaction and name-calling
jokes.explanation: Standard Ross jealousy escalation, predictable but in character

jokes.text: Oh, Ross. Come on. He is happily married. His wife just had twins. / Should we send something?
jokes.punchline: Ross's social obligation concern overriding jealousy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment where his social anxiety overrides his romantic paranoia in completely inappropriate way

jokes.text: You know what? This calls for a bottle of Israel's finest.
jokes.punchline: Ross suggesting the same terrible champagne
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Ross has learned nothing about celebration quality

jokes.text: The job is in Paris.
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating Rachel's great job news
jokes.punchline: Devastating revelation that changes everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic bombshell that recontextualizes all the celebration, delivered with maximum economy and impact

jokes.text: But you know what? I'm gonna put you on with my bear. Hold on. Hey, Bailey, I need some career advice.
jokes.setup: Joey contemplating his career in talking head segment
jokes.punchline: Consulting his stuffed animal for professional guidance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey absurdity that's both childish and somehow logical within his character framework

tmdb_overview: Monica and Chandler tour a potential new house while Joey tries discouraging their move. Phoebe legally changes her name, Ross gets tenure, and Rachel finds new career opportunities.
